content.content:
=== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6F86xc ===
PATH: crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/mod.rs:1080
DBID: 3330497197

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Gate close effects on an actual open state**

When a queued/stale `SelectDate` arrives after an uncontrolled picker has already closed (for example a calendar click racing with `FocusOut`), `should_close` is still true even though `state` is `Closed`, so this emits `OpenChange` and `RestoreFocusToInput` without any open-state transition. That can call `on_open_change(false)` spuriously and pull focus back into the input after focus has left; only add these close effects when the selection actually closes an open picker.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

=== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6F86xe ===
PATH: crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/mod.rs:145
DBID: 3330497199

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Add a value-change callback to Props**

`ValueChange` is emitted for typed and calendar selections, but unlike `OpenChange` there is no `Props::on_value_change` for the adapter to call. In controlled `value` mode this leaves the parent with no public callback channel to learn `Context::requested_value`, so user selections and clears can be recorded internally without any way for consumers to echo the new value back.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

=== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6F86xg ===
PATH: crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/mod.rs:548
DBID: 3330497201

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Normalize localized digits before parsing**

For locales where users enter native digits (for example Arabic-Indic or Extended Arabic-Indic numerals), this ASCII-only check classifies an otherwise valid date as partial before `parse_date` can commit it. With an existing value, typing a localized numeric date leaves the old selected date and hidden input in place even though the visible field changed; normalize digits with the existing i18n helper before the completeness and parse checks.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

=== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6F86xi ===
PATH: crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/mod.rs:988
DBID: 3330497204

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Let readonly pickers open for viewing**

When `readonly` is true, this guard blocks the trigger and ArrowDown open paths, and the focus path is also gated elsewhere, so a read-only picker cannot open the calendar at all. That contradicts the prop contract that read-only allows viewing but not editing; keep the input/selection mutation guards, but allow opening so users can inspect the current date/month without changing the value.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

input.description: Check parity reference for readonly semantics

content.text: The calendar sibling confirms the pattern: a readonly calendar still renders/opens but blocks *selection* (`if ctx.readonly { return None }` only on mutation). So readonly = "view, don't edit." This supports Codex — but the date-picker spec explicitly contradicts itself (Props doc says "viewing," state machine blocks opening with deliberate comments + tests). Since this reverses deliberate, tested behavior, I'll confirm the direction with you:

questions.question: Codex flags that a readonly DatePicker can't open its calendar at all. The Props doc says readonly "allows viewing but not editing" and the sibling Calendar lets a readonly calendar render but blocks selection — supporting Codex. But the date-picker state machine deliberately blocks opening (Open/ArrowDown/FocusIn guards) with passing tests enforcing it. How should I resolve this?

options.label: Allow opening, block editing (Recommended)

Update the spec + flip the readonly_blocks_open tests. Matches the Props doc, the Calendar sibling, and Codex.

options.label: Keep blocking open
options.description: Treat the current behavior as intended (readonly fully prevents opening), push back on Codex with the rationale, and instead fix the Props doc to say readonly prevents opening. No state-machine change.

content.content:
1	//! `spec compile-snippets` — parse Rust code blocks in spec markdown
2	//! through `syn::parse_file` and surface syntax errors.
3	//!
4	//! ## What this catches
5	//!
6	//! - Top-level Rust syntax bugs in spec snippets: missing braces,
7	//!   mistyped `pub fn`, unterminated string literals, unbalanced
8	//!   generics outside macro bodies, etc.
9	//! - Coverage across the full spec corpus —
10	//!   `spec/{components,foundation,leptos-components,dioxus-components,shared,testing}/`.
11	//!   The pre-existing `lint-code` linter only walked
12	//!   `spec/components/` and never invoked a Rust parser, so foundation
13	//!   and per-adapter specs had zero parse coverage.
14	//!
15	//! ## What this **does not** catch (important — read this)
16	//!
17	//! - **Macro-internal syntax.** `view! { … }` and `rsx! { … }` are
18	//!   opaque token trees to both `syn` and `rustc`; everything inside
19	//!   the outer braces is unchecked. The original Leptos `view!`
20	//!   regression (an inline `|errors: ArcRwSignal<Errors>|` closure
21	//!   where the macro parser misread `<Errors>` as an HTML tag) sits
22	//!   *inside* the macro and would not be caught here. The unit test
23	//!   suites at `crates/ars-{leptos,dioxus}/tests/` are the source of
24	//!   truth for macro-internal correctness.
25	//! - **Type / path resolution.** A snippet that names a type defined
26	//!   nowhere in the workspace still parses syntactically. Use
27	//!   `cargo check` against a fixture crate for that.
28	//! - **Borrow-checker / ownership errors.** Same as above —
29	//!   `syn::parse_file` is purely a parser.
30	//!
31	//! ## Opting out
32	//!
33	//! Code blocks that intentionally contain partial or pseudo-Rust
34	//! (signatures shown for documentation purposes, top-level expressions
35	//! meant to live inside an outer fn body, etc.) opt out by tagging the
36	//! fence info-string with `rust,no_check` (also recognised:
37	//! `rust,ignore`, `rust,no_run`, mirroring rustdoc). The extractor
38	//! honors those markers and skips the block.
39	//!
40	//! ## Performance
41	//!
42	//! Fast: no compiler invocation. Runs in milliseconds across all 2000+
43	//! Rust blocks in the spec, so it is suitable for inclusion in the
44	//! `cargo xci` pipeline (see `xtask::ci::Step::SpecCompileSnippets`).
45

content.text: The cfg gate landed in the pass-7 commit. All four modified files are my pass-8 + CI fixes. Let me commit:

input.command:
cd /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-c59961ed
git add -A
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(date_picker,i18n): address Codex review #697 pass 8 + CI fixes

CI fixes (merged main brought the CRAP gate + feature-flag matrix):
- Gate `date_order_uses_icu_order_*` test on `all(std, icu4x)` so the
  `--no-default-features --features icu4x` matrix job compiles (DateOrder /
  date_order are only imported under that cfg).
- Add a direct `fallback_date_order` unit test. The pass-7 fix made
  `icu_date_order` succeed for native-digit locales, so the heuristic fallback
  lost the incidental coverage those probes gave it (CRAP 5 -> 30 at 0%); the
  direct test restores 100% coverage.

Codex pass 8 (4x P2):
- Gate the SelectDate close effects (`OpenChange`/`RestoreFocusToInput`) and
  close-side context mutations on the picker actually being open, so a stale
  selection racing with an outside-close/focus-out can't fire on_open_change
  spuriously or pull focus back into the input.
- Add `Props::on_value_change` (the value counterpart of `on_open_change`);
  `Context::requested_value` already documented this wiring but the callback
  was missing, leaving controlled-`value` parents no echo channel.
- Normalize native decimal digits in `parse_date`/`classify_input` so a
  localized numeric date (e.g. Arabic-Indic) commits instead of being
  misclassified as in-progress text.
- Let `readonly` pickers open for viewing: drop the readonly guards on
  Open/FocusIn and keep the trigger enabled, while keeping the SelectDate /
  InputChange editing guards and the disabled clear-trigger. Matches the Props
  doc ("viewing but not editing") and the Calendar sibling.

Spec synced in spec/components/date-time/date-picker.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git log --oneline -1
